COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY APPELLANT KIDANE, ABREHA

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. APPELLANT ABREHA KIDANE'S MOTION SET ASIDE ORDER DISMISSING CASE IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF ABREHA KIDANE ("KIDANE") WAS SERVED WITH THE ORDER, DECISION, OR AWARD OF THE LABOR COMMISSIONER IN MAY 2013. (DECLARATION OF ABREHA KIDANE, PAR. 26.) KIDANE FILED A NOTICE OF APPEAL ON MAY 23, 2013. KIDANE ALSO FILED A MOTION FOR WAIVER OF THE UNDERTAKING REQUIREMENT, WHICH WAS DENIED ON JULY 10, 2013. EVEN ASSUMING THAT THE TIME TO POST THE UNDERTAKING WAS STAYED UNTIL THE DETERMINATION OF KIDANE'S MOTION FOR WAIVER, THE LATEST KIDANE COULD HAVE POSTED THE UNDERTAKING FOR THE COURT TO OBTAIN JURISDICTION FOR THE APPEAL WAS 10 DAYS FROM ENTRY OF THAT ORDER, OR JULY 24, 2013. (LAB. CODE SEC. 98.2(B).) THE COURT DOES NOT HAVE THE AUTHORITY TO EXTEND THE DEADLINE FOR POSTING THE STATUTORILY MANDATED UNDERTAKING. (PALAGIN V. PANIAGUA CONSTRUCTION, INC. (2013) 222 CAL.APP.4TH 124, 140.) THEREFORE, THE MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE ORDER DISMISSING THE APPEAL FILED ON OCTOBER 16, 2013 IS DENIED. DEFENDANT'S REQUESTS TO REINSTATE A JUDGMENT OR SET ASIDE A STAY ARE DENIED AT THIS TIME. DEFENDANT MAY FILE A SEPARATE MOTION FOR THIS RELIEF IF HE WISHES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR FILED BY APPELLANT KIDANE, ABREHA

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. APPELLANT ABREHA KIDANE'S MOTION TO WAIVE UNDERTAKING (BOND OR CASH) REQUIREMENT IS DENIED. MR. KIDANE HAS NOT SUFFICIENTLY DEMONSTRATED INDIGENCE OR INABILITY TO OBTAIN SUFFICIENT SURETIES TO WAIVE THE BOND REQUIREMENT OF LABOR CODE 98.2(B). (CCP 995.240.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185.
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR ASSIGNEE THE NATIONAL COLLECTION AGENCY, INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO POST UNDERTAKING HEARING SET FOR AUG-29-2013 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, ASSIGNEE THE NATIONAL COLLECTION AGENCY, INC.'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO POST UNDERTAKING IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION WAS FILED. ON MAY 25, 2013, MR. KIDANE TIMELY FILED WITH THE COURT A NOTICE OF APPEAL. "AS A CONDITION TO FILING AN APPEAL PURSUANT TO THIS SECTION, AN EMPLOYER SHALL FIRST POST AN UNDERTAKING WITH THE REVIEWING COURT IN THE AMOUNT OF THE ORDER, DECISION, OR AWARD. THE UNDERTAKING SHALL CONSIST OF AN APPEAL BOND ISSUED BY A LICENSED SURETY OR A CASH DEPOSIT WITH THE COURT IN THE AMOUNT OF THE ORDER, DECISION, OR AWARD." (LAB. CODE 98.2(B).) ON JUNE 13, 2013, MR. KIDANE FILED A MOTION TO WAIVE UNDERTAKING (BOND OR CASH) REQUIREMENT OF LABOR CODE 98.2(B), AND ON JULY 10, 2013, THE COURT DENIED MR. KIDANE'S MOTION. MR. KIDANE HAS NOT FILED AN UNDERTAKING AND HAS NOT OPPOSED THIS MOTION. THEREFORE, THIS ACTION IS DISMISSED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

calendar.matters:
Mtn Tpo Set Aside Order Dismissing Case
Off Calendar May-08-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Fred Keeperman, Esq. of the Law Offices of Fred Keeperman, Tel. 925.631.7710, for assignee The National Collection Agency;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was not contested. APPELLANT ABREHA KIDANE's Motion Set Aside Order Dismissing Case is denied. Plaintiff Abreha Kidane ("Kidane") was served with the Order, Decision, or Award of the Labor Commissioner in May 2013. (Declaration of Abreha Kidane, par. 26.) Kidane filed a Notice of Appeal on May 23, 2013. Kidane also filed a motion for waiver of the undertaking requirement, which was denied on July 10, 2013. Even assuming that the time to post the undertaking was stayed until the determination of Kidane's motion for waiver, the latest Kidane could have posted the undertaking for the Court to obtain jurisdiction for the appeal was 10 days from entry of that order, or July 24, 2013. (Lab. Code sec. 98.2(b).) The court does not have the authority to extend the deadline for posting the statutorily mandated undertaking. (Palagin v. Paniagua Construction, Inc. (2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 124, 140.) Therefore, the motion to set aside the Order dismissing the appeal filed on October 16, 2013 is denied. Defendant's requests to reinstate a judgment or set aside a stay are denied at this time. Defendant may file a separate motion for this relief if he wishes. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Assignee The National Collection Agency, Inc.'S Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Post Undertaking
Off Calendar Aug-29-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter.

RULING; The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. Assignee The National Collection Agency, Inc.'s Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Post Undertaking is granted. No opposition was filed. On May 25, 2013, Mr. Kidane timely filed with the Court a Notice of Appeal. "As a condition to filing an appeal pursuant to this section, an employer shall first post an undertaking with the reviewing court in the amount of the order, decision, or award. The undertaking shall consist of an appeal bond issued by a licensed surety or a cash deposit with the court in the amount of the order, decision, or award." (Lab. Code 98.2(b).) On June 13, 2013, Mr. Kidane filed a motion to waive undertaking (bond or cash) requirement of Labor Code 98.2(b), and on July 10, 2013, the Court denied Mr. Kidane's motion. Mr. Kidane has not filed an undertaking and has not opposed this motion. Therefore, this action is dismissed. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Notice Of Motion To Waive Undertaking (Bond Or Cash) Requirement
Off Calendar Jul-10-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: ATTY FRED KEEPERMAN, 925.631.7710, APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF ELMER TEP CHAN;
ATTY JOHN BROWN, 415.409.8600, APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT ABREHA KIDANE.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. APPELLANT ABREHA KIDANE'S Motion To Waive Undertaking (Bond Or Cash) Requirement is Denied. Mr. Kidane has not sufficiently demonstrated indigence or inability to obtain sufficient sureties to waive the bond requirement of Labor Code 98.2(b). (CCP 995.240.) Order signed. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185.
